compiled_state.title: As the RED test evaluator, I align my rejection criteria with the shared behavioral testing standard
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the RED test evaluator (dso:red-test-evaluator) prompt to align with the shared behavioral testing standard, adding refactoring survival and suite-level value as rejection criteria.
**Why**: The evaluator currently uses its own criteria that may diverge from the shared standard, leading to inconsistent test quality signals.
**Scope**:
- IN: RED test evaluator consumes the shared behavioral testing standard
- IN: Add refactoring survival as a rejection criterion (test would break on a behavior-preserving refactor)
- IN: Add suite-level value as a rejection criterion (test duplicates coverage already provided by existing tests)
- OUT: Modifying the RED test writer agent (separate story)
- OUT: Modifying the shared testing standard itself (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the RED test evaluator prompt is updated to align with the shared behavioral testing standard, with refactoring survival and suite-level value as rejection criteria
  ← Satisfies: "The RED test evaluator prompt is updated to align with the shared behavioral testing standard, adding refactoring survival and suite-level value as rejection criteria"
- When this story is complete, the RED test evaluator consumes the shared behavioral testing standard
  ← Satisfies: "All test-writing agent prompts consume a shared behavioral testing standard"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Rejection criteria must reference the shared standard, not duplicate it

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
